updateMessage: Audit fix: Updated IRS progressive brackets to official 2026 Mainland values (OE2026).

incomeTax.name: IRS
incomeTax.tooltip: Imposto sobre o Rendimento de Pessoas Singulares. Progressive tax on personal income for residents in Mainland Portugal. The €4,104 standard deduction is modeled as a pre-bracket adjustment.

standard.tooltip: Standard progressive IRS regime for residents in Mainland Portugal.

nhr_2_0.tooltip: NHR 2.0 (IFICI) regime for high-value activities, applying a 20% flat rate to eligible employment income for 10 years.

socialContributions.name: TSU
socialContributions.tooltip: Taxa Social Única (Employee portion). Flat 11% contribution on gross salary for private sector employees, with no cap.

additionalTaxes.name: TAS
additionalTaxes.tooltip: Taxa Adicional de Solidariedade. Surcharge of 2.5% on taxable income above €80,000 and 5% above €250,000.

capitalGainsTax.name: Mais-valias
capitalGainsTax.tooltip: Flat 28% tax on capital gains from financial assets (stocks, bonds). Residents can optionally choose to aggregate these with other income (not modeled).

propertyGainsTax.tooltip: Property capital gains are taxed as ordinary income. Real-world 50% inclusion rule is a known gap (100% is modeled).

helpText.statePensionDescriptionP1:
Enter your expected monthly Portuguese state pension amount.

Use your own estimate from Segurança Social or your pension statement. The simulator treats this as a monthly amount because Portugal's tax rules define state pension inputs monthly.
helpText.statePensionDescriptionP2:
Enter your partner's expected monthly Portuguese state pension amount.

Use their own estimate from Segurança Social or their pension statement. The simulator treats this as a monthly amount because Portugal's tax rules define state pension inputs monthly.

options.label: Standard Progressive
options.description: Standard progressive IRS regime for residents in Mainland Portugal.

options.label: NHR 2.0 (IFICI) - 20%
options.description: NHR 2.0 (IFICI) regime for high-value activities, applying a 20% flat rate to eligible employment income for 10 years.

investmentTypes.label: Criptoativos
investmentTypes.helpText: Gains on crypto-assets held for less than 365 days are taxed at 28%. The 365-day exemption is not modeled.
